code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: ONLY a single number: 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5
    - Do NOT output any explanation, text, symbols, or punctuation
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or anger
    - Product is defective, unsafe, completely ineffective, or returned
    - Clear statements such as "terrible," "junk," "don't buy," "returned," "doesn't work"
    - Safety hazards or total failure automatically lean toward 1

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - More negative than positive
    - Significant flaws, poor quality, missing parts, misleading description
    - Some limited usefulness but strong disappointment
    - Language such as "cheap," "useless," "not as pictured," "pain to use"

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros and cons
    - "It's okay," "average," "not great but not terrible"
    - Works but with noticeable limitations
    - Conflicted or moderate tone
    - If sentiment is clearly mixed and neither side dominates, choose 3

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Mostly positive with minor criticisms
    - Product works well but not perfect
    - Phrases like "pretty good," "above average," "would recommend"
    - Minor complaints that do not override satisfaction
    - Positive aspects clearly outweigh negatives but lack enthusiasm

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Strong satisfaction and enthusiasm
    - Clear recommendation to others
    - Exceeds expectations
    - Phrases like "love it," "perfect," "excellent," "extremely happy," "worth every penny"
    - Mentions durability/long-term satisfaction: "held up well," "lasts years"
    - Enthusiastic endorsement despite minor practical considerations

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Mixed sentiment: Weigh overall conclusion more heavily than isolated complaints
    - Updates: Prioritize final evaluation (positive update = maintain higher rating)
    - Sarcasm: Detect implicit negativity through contextual cues
    - Comparison phrasing: "Wouldn't buy again" → 1-2, "Best I've used" → 5
    - Practical drawbacks: Minor maintenance requirements don't negate 5-star if satisfaction is strong
    - Concise praise: Brief but unequivocal positivity ("Great size, works perfectly") → 5
</DynamicRules>

</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: It's not a good vacuum. It's hard to pick up dirt and then it will spit it out so you have to go over and over the same area. I wouldn't buy it again.
Rating: 1

Example 2:
Review: Arrived broken. :(
Rating: 1

Example 3:
Review: The cushions are okay, but the size was waaaay off for my rocker. I'm still using it though because it is better than nothing.
Rating: 3

Example 4:
Review: I'd rate this as pretty good although it doesn't feel quite as heavy duty as the ones we've been using... Overall I'd rate this as above average although only time will tell how durable it is.
Rating: 4

Example 5:
Review: This is a sharp well made tablecloth. Strengths are vibrant colors and solid construction. The cons are just needing to iron cotton.
Rating: 5

Example 6:
Review: EDIT: A year later still works great! Sometimes slow to adjust but no real issues. Very satisfied long-term.
Rating: 5

Example 7:
Review: These are really nice washcloths - soft, thick, and hold up well in wash. Only minor shrinkage after first laundry.
Rating: 5

Example 8:
Review: Makes excellent coffee with shower-head water distribution. Carafe keeps it hot, though handle feels slightly awkward.
Rating: 4

Example 9:
Review: Great size, convenient for one-skillet meals. Does exactly what I needed without any fuss.
Rating: 5

Example 10:
Review: The fabric feels cheap and stitching came loose after 2 washes. Not worth the price at all.
Rating: 2

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (32 total misclassified):
  gold 5 -> 4: 15
  gold 4 -> 5: 13
  gold 4 -> 3: 2
  gold 2 -> 1: 1
  gold 1 -> 2: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "This is a coffee pot for coffee geeks.  Most discerning coffee aficionados likely wouldn't notice much if any difference from a a great less complicated coffee make, such as a Bonavita.  To cut to the point, this probably isn't for you, but who knows..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I wanted to try Midnight Scoop because it promised to be easy on the wrists and joints and it is. To use it you must learn a new way of scooping and to hold it differently. It is held at the back end then pushed forward in a straight line. There is. ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "What to say about a filter? It arrived in good time and appears to be flawless."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "I hate to review these towels as 3 stars, but the particular set I have (Skeleton Applique) is just that. Actually they are more of a 2 star set, but I'll explain my 3 star rating later on.

The gold and black striped towels are not absorbent at all ..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [2, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "Nice, bright napkins for your inside or outside dining. You get six napkins and they are a generous 20x20. They are also 100% cotton. They are well sewn and the print is only on one side. These are machine washable, but they require a low heat to dry..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "This is a 100% cotton runner that is fantastic for a sweetheart, anniversary or Valentine's Day dinner. It feels like a table napkin. It is a bit on the thin side. After using and washing, you may get shrinkage if you use warm or hot water and stick ..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 3, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 32 errors, 12 borderline out of 80 examples."}
